name: lustre
explanation: The Lustre check shells out to local Lustre administrative binaries — `lctl` (default `/usr/sbin/lctl`), `lnetctl` (`/usr/sbin/lnetctl`), and `lfs` (`/usr/bin/lfs`) — to enumerate devices, parse params, collect LNET stats, and (on clients) read changelogs. There is no network endpoint and no credential. All instance fields are optional with sensible defaults: `node_type` is auto-detected from `lctl dl` output (mds if any `mdt`, oss if any `ost`, otherwise client), and `filesystems` defaults to discovery via `lctl`. Discovery is host-local: "are the Lustre user-space binaries installed and is this host a Lustre client/MDS/OSS?". This matches pattern L (Local CLI subprocess), with the additional wrinkle that some collection paths (changelogs, MDS/OSS jobstats) require root or a registered changelog user — environmental constraints, not credentials to discover.
notes: Pattern L. Method `other`: discovery is presence/runnability of `lctl` (and node-type self-detection from its output). Optional changelog collection on clients additionally needs a registered changelog user, which the auto-config layer cannot provision.
